Authors are listed alphabetically by surname for each chapter;
ordering does not indicate the level of individual contributions. The
initial draft of the technical report was based on research conducted by
KME, which was further developed through investigations by MLN, SBO, and
DAR. All authors contributed additional research, writing, editing, or
feedback for their respective chapters. DJB and JMS coordinated the
compilation and review of the final report.

Acknowledgments
The authors of this report would like to thank the co-authors of the Science article for providing input at various stages of the technical report’s creation. In addition, we are grateful to the following people who provided critical feedback on drafts of individual chapters, or, in some cases, the entire report: William J. Bradshaw, Salvadore Buse, Ivana Cvijović, John Donnelly, Seth Donoughe, Steven Erickson, Julius Fredens, Louise F.H. Funke, Jacob Glanville, Alex Kleinman, Michael McLaren, Alexander Mijalis, Sriharshita (Harshu) Musunuri, Marta Napiorkowska, Velislava Petrova, Georgia Ray, Daniel Rice, Adin Richards, Maximilian Schons, Jon Sanders, Elizabeth Shepherd, Chris Somerville, Christopher Stamper, Benjamin Stewart, Gina Suh, Joe Torres, Kyle Webster, James H.R. Wheeler, and Heather Youngs. In addition, we would like to thank Andrew Snyder-Beattie and James Wagstaff, who helped to initiate the writing of the report; and James A. Smith and Ivana Cvijović, who provided operational support in the production and review of the report. Finally, we would like to thank all of the reviewers (listed on pg iii), including those who chose to remain anonymous. Responsibility for the final content rests entirely with the authors.

Figures and illustrations presented in this report were based on
concepts designed by the authors, and created in partnership with Alana
McPherson and Kip Lyall at Science Journal Editors (SJE). The images are
made available under a Creative Commons
Attribution-Noncommercial-Sharealike License (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0).

The authors acknowledge Open Philanthropy for their support of the
technical report through operational assistance, and funding to RKD,
KME, GL, MLN, SBO, and JMS. DAR also acknowledges past support for work
on the same topic. The authors also acknowledge operational support from
the Mirror Biology Dialogues Fund, enabled by financial contributions
from the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation, the David and Lucile Packard
Foundation, the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation, Open Philanthropy,
and Patrick Collison. KPA and JIG acknowledge support from the National
Science Foundation, Award #1901145 (RCN Build-a-Cell: An Open Community
Considering & Advancing the Construction of Synthetic Cells). KME
acknowledges support from NSF Award #1943141 (Towards Open and
Responsive Ecological Editing). YC acknowledges support from UKRI
Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council EP/V05967X/1 (Open
Plus Fellowship: Engineering and safeguarding synthetic genomes). Other
authors acknowledge general support from a wide range of sources. The
views expressed here are those of the individuals and not those of any
organizations with which they are affiliated.

VSC is a co-founder, Scientific Advisor, and equity holder in SeqCoast Genomics, LLC, and Middle Author Bioinformatics, LLC. VSC and JP are board members of the Mirror Biology Dialogues Fund. FJI is a co-founder of Pearl Bio. JDGJ is the founder and a shareholder of Norfolk Plant Sciences and Norfolk Healthy Produce. RM is a member of the advisory board for Cell, and the scientific advisory boards of Amgen, GlaxoSmithKline, and Janssen Pharmaceuticals. SBO is the founder and an executive at SynX Therapeutics Ltd. HS is listed as a co-inventor on patents pertaining to lactazole engineering, as well as patents pertaining to tRNA engineering to enable the incorporation of various D-amino acids. JMS is a former employee and shareholder of Teiko.bio. In the last 5 years, RKD, KME, TVI, GL, MLN, SBO, JP, DAR, JMS, and BW have, either as individuals or as project leaders, received funding from Open Philanthropy to support work that is not directly related to this report. KPA, DA, YC, TWH, JIG, REL, JWS, and NJT declare no conflicts of interest relevant to this research
